description: Revamp a frontend page or module using the configured design guides and reference modules while keeping the requested behavior constraints.
request: Revamp the public static and legal route family in Huz-Web-Frontend so About Us, Core Values, How We Work, HajjUmrah.co Business, Careers, Travel Articles/Travel Essentials, Visa Services, FAQs, Terms & Conditions, Privacy & Cookies, Refund Policy, Cancellation Policy, Contact Us, and Help Center where applicable all match the shared Huz homepage design system and strongest reference pages. Use the bot-owned HUZ workspace as durable memory. Remove demo-like or duplicate content, reduce empty whitespace, align layout/colors/spacing with BrandPageShell and homepage tokens, improve UX hierarchy, and rewrite content so it feels credible and production-ready. Prefer shared frontend architecture and content modules; make backend changes only if truly necessary. Account for existing footer label mismatches such as Travel Articles and Help Center during planning instead of assuming labels already map to the correct route destinations.

phases.title: Load Design Context
phases.goal: Read the design guides, atlas docs, and requested reference modules before making UI decisions.
required: Read all relevant design guides and any user-provided reference pages or modules.
Map the target scope, existing component structure, and dependencies using the atlas docs.
Record the non-negotiable UX constraints and intended visual direction.

phases.title: Plan the Revamp
phases.goal: Create a scoped implementation plan that preserves required behavior while applying the design system intentionally.
required: Define which components, layouts, and shared primitives will change.
Reference the design guides and example modules explicitly in the plan.
Avoid ambiguous redesign drift; keep the requested scope precise.

phases.title: Implement the Revamp
phases.goal: Apply the new frontend design across the requested page or module while keeping behavior intact.
required: Implement the new UI using the design guides and the referenced module/page examples.
Keep routes, data flow, and behavior aligned unless the request explicitly calls for functional changes.
Extract reusable shared UI only when it improves consistency across the target module.

phases.title: Verify UX and Responsiveness
phases.goal: Check the updated pages for visual consistency, responsiveness, and implementation correctness.
required: Run the project build and any relevant frontend checks.
Spot-check the target pages for layout, responsiveness, and regressions.
Adjust the implementation if the result drifts from the requested design direction.

phases.title: Refresh Docs and Summary
phases.goal: Update blueprint and design docs so future runs know how the module changed.
required: Update the atlas docs for the touched module, components, routes, and shared dependencies.
Update the design context doc with the guides and reference examples used.
Append a final implementation summary and verification notes.
